title: Rubroshorea ovalis
intro: Rubroshorea ovalis, also known as the "Oval-leaved Shorea," is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Rubroshorea ovalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Rubroshorea ovalis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
